title: Can Australia afford free childcare? | Alan Kohler | ABC NEWS
description: The prime minister has signalled the federal government will use the May budget to deliver urgent reform amid growing global uncertainty and cost-of-living pressures. ABC’s Alan Kohler has one late suggestion for the treasurer: nationalise the childcare industry and make it free by extending public education to kids under 5. title: India's first census in fifteen years could shift power within the country | ABC NEWS
description: The world’s largest population count has begun in India as government officials criss-cross the nation to collect data on living conditions and classes.  Questions will be included in this survey regarding the proportion of different castes in the population which haven't been asked since the end of British administration.  The University of Melbourne’s Dr Pradeep Taneja says a larger lower caste would want a greater voice on India’s socio-economic and political matters. title: The volunteers reviving Sydney's Cooks River from toxic pollution | ABC NEWS
description: Winding its way through Sydney's inner western suburbs, the 23-kilometre Cooks River was considered an industrial dump for much of the 20th century. There are now hundreds of volunteers, dubbed the "mudcrabs", who give their time to help clean, maintain and repair a catchment which is home to about half a million — or about 10 per cent — of Sydney's residents. title: The fuel crisis impact on the Philippines' poorest | ABC NEWS
description: As the global energy crisis deepens as a result of the Iran war, oil-importing nations in South-East Asia are facing escalating economic pain.   The Philippines is at the frontline of the crisis, now declaring a national energy emergency.   There are fears sky-rocketing fuel prices is feeding economy-wide inflation, raising the cost of food for the country's poorest.   Video by Tim Swanston. title: Hundreds of anti-war protesters take to the streets of Tel Aviv | ABC NEWS
description: Hundreds of anti-war protesters have rallied in Tel Aviv on Saturday, despite Israeli wartime restrictions currently limit gatherings in public areas to 150 people.  Supreme Court justices issued an interim ruling saying at least 600 people would be allowed to gather at Habima Square and 150 at each of several other locations across the country.  📸 via ABC News correspondent Allyson Horn.
